title: Nursery Rhyme. CXCVII. Riddles.
content:
There was a king met a king
            In a narrow lane,
        Says this king to that king,
            "Where have you been?"

        "Oh! I've been a hunting
            With my dog and my doe."
        "Pray lend him to me,
            That I may do so."

        "There's the dog take the dog."
            "What's the dog's name?"
        "I've told you already."
            "Pray tell me again."
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. CXCVII. Riddles."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
